Creating a Windows Install USB From macOS
Need to reinstall Windows but only have a Mac? Making a Windows 10 install USB on macOS — ExFAT vs MS-DOS, where to grab the ISO, and writing the image.
Did You Know? Windows' Shutdown Isn't Actually Shutting Down
Newer Windows versions ship with 'Fast Startup' on by default — Shutdown actually dumps system state to a file and reloads it next boot. Why, and how to turn it off.
Tired of Windows 10 Forcing Updates on You? Here's How to Disable Auto-Updates

Windows 10's forced update behavior is the worst — slowing your machine mid-work, throwing random new bugs, or worst of all bluescreen on a botched update. This post walks through disabling the Windows Update service from the Services UI. Heads up — this also disables security updates, so only do it if you understand the security trade-off.
Windows "Could Not Find This Item" — When a File or Folder Refuses to Delete

Recently hit a familiar Windows annoyance — try to delete a file or folder, and Windows says "Could not find this item". I've been seeing this since Windows 7, and it's still around on Windows 10. This post covers the two common causes (a process holding the file open vs. disk sector trouble) and the easy ways to fix each.
Expose a Local Server to the Internet With ngrok (Windows + macOS Setup)

ngrok is a handy way to expose a local dev server to the internet — roughly 5 minutes of setup gets you a public HTTPS URL that forwards to your localhost. The first-run setup has a few quirks (especially on macOS — Gatekeeper blocks the binary; PATH needs configuring), so this post walks through Windows + macOS setup end to end. Useful for sharing a local prototype, testing webhooks, or wiring up a Line Bot without deploying.
Fixing 'npm WARN config global --global, --local are deprecated. Use --location=global instead'
After updating to Node.js 16.15.1 and npm 8.11.0, I kept seeing "npm WARN config global --global, --local are deprecated. Use --location=global instead". Here's a note on the fix.
How to Clear the Terminal Screen (Mac and Windows)

Whether you're on Mac or Windows, the terminal piles up output fast and it gets noisy. Quick note on the commands and keyboard shortcuts that clear the screen — including the distinction between "actually wiping scrollback" vs. "just scrolling the cursor down so the screen looks empty".
nvm on Windows: Fixing "Access is denied" and "exit status 145"

Two common nvm errors on Windows — "nvm use exit status 1: Access is denied" and "exit status 145: The directory is not empty." Quick notes on both — the first is almost always a permissions issue (run as administrator), and the second is usually a leftover nodejs folder from a previous install that nvm can't overwrite.
Window Went Off-Screen on Mac or Windows? How to Pull It Back

Sometimes a window drifts off your screen and you can't grab it with the mouse. This post covers two quick fixes — Alt + Space → Move + arrow keys on Windows to drag any off-screen window back, and the Window → Zoom menu (or force-quit) on Mac.
Windows "running scripts is disabled on this system" — How to Fix PowerShell Execution Policy

Hit "running scripts is disabled on this system, so cannot load..." in Windows PowerShell? Windows defaults the Execution Policy to Restricted (especially on Windows 10 PowerShell), which blocks script execution as a security measure. This post explains the Execution Policy levels (AllSigned, Bypass, Default, RemoteSigned, Restricted, Undefined, Unrestricted) and how to switch to RemoteSigned so npm, vue, gulp, webpack, etc. work normally.
